title: Microsoft 365 Copilot pricing
decisionQuestion: What list price to set for Copilot for Microsoft 365 commercial customers?
situation: Copilot is broadly available for E3, E5, Business Standard, and Business Premium customers; Amy Hood's policy is to list price products adding significant value.
constraints: Must align with Amy's value-based list price policy

options.description: USD30 per user per month for E3, E5, Business Standard, and Business Premium customers
options.expectedBenefit: Monetizes productivity improvements at a premium tier
options.principalRisk: Customers may resist the additional monthly cost

options.description: Include Copilot at no extra cost with existing E3/E5 licenses
options.principalRisk: Leaves significant value uncaptured from productivity gains

rejectedBenefit: Increased retention from bundling at no extra cost
observations: Amy stated list pricing applies when adding a lot of value
inferences: The $30 price reflects the belief that Copilot delivers substantial productivity value

evidenceSpans.exactQuote: Copilot for Microsoft 365 will be available for commercial customers for USD30 per user per month for Microsoft 365 E3, E5, Business Standard, and Business Premium customers when broadly available
when we believe we’re adding a lot of value, and frankly, that’s what the copilots are doing and some productivity improvement, you can expect that we will have a list price for those.
evidenceSpans.speaker: Amy Hood
